rows.llm_rationale: As of May 2026, we're in the latter half of the year with limited remaining Fed meetings (typically 2 FOMC meetings remain). Historical base rates show rate cuts occur during economic slowdowns or crises; current conditions would need to deteriorate significantly in 5-7 months for a cut to materialize. The crowd price of 15.5% aligns reasonably with CME FedWatch implied probabilities for this scenario, reflecting low but non-negligible recession/slowdown probability. Slight upward adjustment to 16% accounts for tail risks (unexpected economic weakness, geopolitical shock) that markets may underweight near expiry.
rows.question: Will 1 Fed rate cut happen in 2026?
